title: Sparse Matrix-Vector Multiplication
description:
Multiply a sparse matrix by a dense vector.

The matrix is represented as a dictionary mapping (row, col) tuples to numeric values. Entries not present in the dictionary are zero.

Rules:
- shape is a tuple (rows, cols). Both rows and cols must be positive.
- vec must have length exactly equal to cols.
- All matrix keys must be within bounds (0 <= row < rows, 0 <= col < cols).
- Raise ValueError for invalid shapes, mismatched vector length, or out-of-bounds keys.
- Return a list of length rows containing the product (floats are acceptable).
function_signature: def sparse_matvec(matrix: dict[tuple[int, int], float], shape: tuple[int, int], vec: list[float]) -> list[float]:
